bib.title: Fast string searching in secondary storage: Theoretical developments and experimental results
bib.citation: Proceedings of the seventh annual ACM-SIAM symposium on Discrete algorithms …, 1996
bib.author: Paolo Ferragina and Roberto Grossi
bib.abstract: In a previous work [S], we proposed a text indexing data structure for secondary storage, which we called SB-tree, that combines the best of B-trees and suffix arrays, overcoming the limitations of inverted files, suffix arrays, suffix trees, and prefix B-trees. In this paper we study the performance of SB-trees in a practical setting, performing a set of searching and updating experiments. Improved performance was obtained by a new space efficient and alphabet-independent organization of the internal nodes of the SB-tree, and a new batch insertion procedure that avoids thrashing.
